items.title: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico, but import taxes still in place for Canada and China
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump holds off his tariff threats against Mexico for one month after President Claudia Sheinbaum agrees to send 10,000 national guard troops to the border. Trump’s tariffs against Canada and China are still slated to go into effect Tuesday.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Netanyahu heads to US to discuss 'victory over Hamas' with Trump
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Donald Trump's meeting with Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday will be his first with a foreign leader since returning to the White House. It comes as U.S. and Arab mediators begin the daunting work of brokering the next phase of a ceasefire agreement to wind down the 15-month war in Gaza. The AP's Joe Federman explains.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday during a press briefing that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump the planned tariffs are on hold. #shorts</article>

items.title: American bald eagles are having a moment, ecologically and culturally
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>America’s national bird, the bald eagle, is having a moment. The eagles are in a sort of environmental updraft since the early 2000’s when the federal government took the thriving birds off its endangered species list, with more states following suit. (AP Video: Seth Wenig/Mike Catalini) Read more here: https://bit.ly/4gj9CA4

#usa #baldeagle #news

items.title: Trump's tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China explained
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs against Canada and Mexico led to retaliatory taxes by both countries on U.S. imports. Trump said the tariffs on the two neighboring countries were designed to stop illegal immigration and the illicit fentanyl trade. He also placed an additional 10% tariff on imports from China with the stated goal of stopping the production of fentanyl. The AP's Cara Rubinsky explains. Read more: https://bit.ly/40XIbXY

#tariffs #trump #mexico #china #canada #usa #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.content:

And then it happened — an absolute blockbuster.

items.title: Pennsylvania grocery shoppers react to Trump's tariff order
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Shoppers at a supermarket in rural town of Punxsutawney, Pennsylvania reacted on Sunday to a move by U.S. President Donald Trump a day earlier to impose tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico and China — America's three biggest trading partners. His tariffs against Canada and Mexico in order to stop illegal immigration and the illicit fentanyl trade have led to retaliatory taxes by both countries on U.S. imports. items.content:

items.title: Hundreds reach out to catch lucky beans at Japan's annual festival
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Residents of downtown Tokyo descended on a Buddhist temple to mark the last day of winter in the Japanese lunar calendar by having dried soybeans, rice cakes and other snacks thrown at them. The traditional event is said to drive away evil spirits and bring good luck. (AP video by Mayuko Ono)

items.title: Hospitals overwhelmed in Congo's Goma nearly a week after rebels took over
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>At least 773 people were killed in the eastern city of Goma and its vicinity this week amid fighting with Rwanda-backed rebels who captured the city in a major escalation of a decadelong conflict, Congolese authorities said Saturday. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4aFInyk

#congo #africa #news

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>AP is live from the 2025 Grammys red carpet in Los Angeles. 

Beyoncé, Taylor Swift, Billie Eilish, Kendrick Lamar, Sabrina Carpenter, Charli xcx, Chappell Roan and André 3000 are among the 2025 nominees that could take home trophies at the 67th annual Grammy Awards. Follow along for live Grammys updates: https://bit.ly/4gujMOm 

#grammys #grammyawards #live 
Courtesy: Recording Academy</article>

items.title: Netanyahu departs for the US to meet Trump
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is flying to Washington on Sunday for a visit in which he will meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, his office said Saturday. Trump invited Netanyahu to the White House last week, making the Israeli premier the first foreign leader to meet the American president in his second term. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3WHaiIo

items.content:

items.content:

items.content:

items.content:

items.title: Gaza ceasefire sees smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages and Palestinian prisoners
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Three men held hostage in the Gaza Strip for more than 15 months were freed Saturday, bringing to 18 the number of captives released by militants since the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas began Jan. 19. In the ceasefire’s first phase, a total of 33 hostages in Gaza will be released in exchange for almost 2,000 Palestinian prisoners held by Israel. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3WINdFf

#israel #gaza #news

items.content:

items.title: Arizona appoints first Latina and Black justice to state Supreme Court
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Arizona Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs has appointed Maria Elena Cruz to the Arizona Supreme Court. The state appellate judge from rural Yuma County will become the first Latina and the first Black person chosen for the state’s high court.

#shorts #arizona #supremecourt</article>

items.title: Serbia protesters blockade Danube bridges to mark 3 months since canopy collapse killed 15
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Many in Serbia believe that the collapse was essentially caused by government corruption in a large infrastructure project with Chinese state companies. Critics believe graft led to a sloppy job during the reconstruction of the Novi Sad train station, poor oversight and disrespect of existing safety regulations. The issue has come to symbolize a wider discontent over the rule of law in Serbia. (AP video shot by Vojislav Stjepanovic) Read more here: https://bit.ly/3WH17rq

#serbia #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>An Italian navy ship Saturday took back to Italy the 43 migrants from the asylum processing centers in Albania following a court decision in Rome in the third failed attempt by Giorgia Meloni’s far-right government to process migrants in the nearby Western Balkan country. Read more here: https://bit.ly/4jCiQKx

items.title: Released Palestinian prisoners greeted in West Bank with cheers and tears
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>25 of the more than 180 Palestinian prisoners freed on Saturday arrived in the occupied West Bank. all were serving long or life sentences in prison, many for involvement in attacks against Israelis. They were greeted with cheers of welcome and tears of joy as they stepped off the Red Cross bus into jostling crowds of well-wishers in the Palestinian town of Betunia, near Ramallah. (AP video by Amer Abdeen; Produced by Jalal Bwaitel). Read more: https://bit.ly/3WINdFf

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Several people died overnight as Russian drone and missile strikes pounded Ukraine’s towns and cities, local officials said Saturday. The attack prompted emergency power grid shutdowns in seven Ukrainian regions, including Poltava, state energy company Ukrenergo said. Read more: https://bit.ly/3QldAOh

items.title: Sick and wounded Palestinian children prepare for evacuation under Gaza ceasefire deal
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A group of 50 sick and wounded Palestinian children were set to cross to Egypt for treatment through Gaza’s Rafah crossing on Saturday, the first opening of the border since Israel captured it nearly nine months ago. The reopening of the Rafah crossing represents a significant breakthrough that bolsters the ceasefire deal Israel and Hamas agreed to earlier this month.

#gaza #rafah #news #israel #palestinian

items.title: FDA approves painkiller designed to eliminate the risk of addiction associated with opioids
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Federal officials on Thursday approved a new type of pain pill designed to eliminate the risks of addiction and overdose associated with opioid medications like Vicodin and OxyContin.

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration said it approved Vertex Pharmaceuticals’ Journavx for short-term pain that often follows surgery or injuries. Read more: https://bit.ly/42wp9t7 

#fda #oxycontin</article>

items.content:

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Panama President José Raúl Mulino said Thursday there will be no negotiation with the United States over ownership of the Panama Canal, and he hopes U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s upcoming visit will allow them to focus on shared interests including migration and combating drug trafficking. (AP/ Abraham Terán).

#panamacanal #news

items.title: What can a ‘black box’ tell us about a plane crash?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>It’s one of the most important pieces of forensic evidence following a plane crash: The so-called “black box.” There are actually two of these remarkably sturdy devices: the cockpit voice recorder and the flight data recorder. And they’re typically orange, not black. (AP video: Kendria LaFleur). Read more: https://bit.ly/3WHQlBz 

#news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.content:
